name: Value-Based Preference Elicitation (Schwartz PVQ)
core_claim: The Portrait Values Questionnaire (PVQ) operationalises Shalom Schwartz's Theory of Basic Human Values: 10 universal motivational value types (Self-Direction, Stimulation, Hedonism, Achievement, Power, Security, Conformity, Tradition, Benevolence, Universalism) arranged in a circumplex and collapsible into four higher-order dimensions on two orthogonal axes — Openness-to-Change vs Conservation, and Self-Transcendence vs Self-Enhancement. Instead of rating trait adjectives, respondents read short third-person 'portraits' of a person whose goals/aspirations express a value ('It is important to her to think up new ideas and be creative') and rate how much that person is like them, which makes value priorities concrete and less abstract than the older Schwartz Value Survey. Values differ from personality traits in a way that matters for media: traits describe how a person characteristically behaves (Big Five), whereas values describe what a person considers important and worth pursuing — the desirable end-states that guide moral judgement, group identity, and political stance. The claim for media is that narrative fiction is fundamentally about value conflict (whose ends prevail, what is sacrificed, what moral order is affirmed or subverted), so a viewer's value priorities should predict which moral/political narratives feel resonant versus alienating. Self-Transcendence (Universalism/Benevolence) viewers are theorised to gravitate to social-justice, found-family, and humanist narratives; Self-Enhancement (Power/Achievement) viewers to ambition, rise-and-fall, and competition stories; Conservation (Security/Conformity/Tradition) viewers to restored-moral-order, family, and institutional narratives; Openness-to-Change (Self-Direction/Stimulation) viewers to unconventional, boundary-pushing, sensation-rich content. It is positioned as the 'values complement to personality' — orthogonal-ish signal about moral/political narrative taste that the Big Five captures only obliquely.
predictive_validity_for_media: Direct TV/film evidence is thin but the closest analogue — MUSIC genre preference — has a solid, replicated link, and the political/moral-narrative pathway is strongly evidenced indirectly. (1) MUSIC: a large Serbian adolescent study (N=1,358, ages 15-17) cross-tabulated 12 genres against the 10 Schwartz values and found four preference factors with coherent value correlates: 'Sophisticated' (Jazz, R&B, Reggae) positively and strongly tied to Self-Direction and Stimulation, negatively to Security/Conformity/Tradition; 'Rebellious' (Metal, Rock, Punk) positively to Self-Direction/Power/Universalism, negatively to Hedonism/Benevolence; 'Energetic' (Techno, Hip-hop, House) positively to Universalism; 'Conventional' (Pop) positively to Benevolence, negatively to Power/Self-Direction. This is direct values-to-genre evidence (effect sizes not reported in the located summary). [uncertain] (2) POLITICAL/MORAL CONTENT — the strongest pathway: a 20-country cross-cultural study (Caprara, Vecchione, Schwartz et al.) shows Self-Transcendence vs Self-Enhancement and Conservation vs Openness predict ideological self-placement and voting, with political orientation driven mainly by social-focus values. Because political dramas, satire-of-power, revolution, and corruption narratives are saturated with exactly these value conflicts, values should predict appetite for that content better than the Big Five does. This is an inference from the values-to-ideology link, not a measured values-to-political-drama-preference finding. [uncertain] (3) No located study directly measures Schwartz values predicting TV/film thematic preference, completion rate, or viewing enjoyment. Net: moderate, indirect-but-coherent predictive case — proven for music genre, strongly plausible for moral/political narrative via the robust values-to-ideology evidence, unmeasured for completion/enjoyment.
instrument_design: Self-report, third-person portrait format; respondent rates 'How much like you is this person?' on a 6-point scale (6 = Very much like me to 1 = Not like me at all). Main forms: PVQ-40 (the 40-item original referenced in this item's name; ~10 min), PVQ-21 (the short form embedded in every round of the European Social Survey since 2002 — 21 items, two per value plus three for Universalism, measures all 10 values and the 4 higher-order dimensions, ~3-5 min), and PVQ-RR / PVQ-5X (refined-theory versions measuring 19 narrower values, longer). PVQ-21 is the de-facto validated short form, run across 30+ countries in the ESS. Note a recent caveat: a 2025 Journal of Personality Assessment paper questions whether the PVQ-21 can recover Schwartz's REFINED (19-value) structure — it is fine for the 10-value / 4-quadrant resolution but should not be pushed to fine-grained value distinctions. For a sub-2-minute, 6-10 item onboarding the realistic minimum is one portrait per higher-order quadrant (4 items: one each for Openness-to-Change, Conservation, Self-Transcendence, Self-Enhancement) up to one portrait per value pair on the two media-relevant axes (~8 items). At 4-8 items the output is a coarse position on the two value axes — a usable soft prior on moral/political narrative tone, not a measurement with facet resolution or reliable single-value scores. [uncertain] (the 4-8 item media-tuned subset is an engineering judgement, not a published validated short form.)
cold_start_applicability: Moderate-to-good and well-suited to cold start in mechanism: it is pure self-report requiring zero viewing history, so it yields signal before the first interaction. Its distinctive cold-start value over the Big Five is orientation on the MORAL and POLITICAL narrative axes (Self-Transcendence vs Self-Enhancement; Conservation vs Openness) — exactly the dimensions the Big Five captures only weakly (Openness overlaps Openness-to-Change; the moral/political axis is largely outside Big Five). For cross-medium inference the value prior should transfer well: a Self-Transcendence-heavy viewer's appetite for humanist/social-justice narrative is medium-agnostic and should carry from a film prior to a TV/anime/K-content catalogue, since value conflicts are universal story material. The forced relevance to HelpME2C's rich moral/power theme cluster (power-corrupts, corruption-and-cover-up, revolution-and-rebellion, moral-compromise) is the standout: values map onto that cluster more directly than any trait model. Signal quality before first interaction: good as a coarse two-axis moral/political-tone prior; weaker for fine genre/structure preference (where Big Five Openness and Sensation-Seeking do more work). Best used as a COMPLEMENT to a personality instrument, not a substitute. [uncertain]
group_compatibility_use: This is values' strongest card for HelpME2C's two-person core use case, and unusually well-evidenced relative to most instruments here. Value congruence between partners is a replicated predictor of relationship quality: a Finnish study of committed couples found similarity in the 10 Schwartz values predicted relationship satisfaction WHILE similarity in Big Five traits did NOT — i.e. for dyads, shared values beat shared personality. Dyadic Actor-Partner Interdependence Model work (van der Wal et al., 2024) confirms actor and partner value effects on relationship quality. The product implication is direct: two partners are more likely to be CONGRUENT on values than random pairs (assortative mating on values is well established), so for the moral/political narrative axes, a joint profile from AVERAGING or INTERSECTION should be confident and rarely conflicted. When partners DIVERGE on a value axis (e.g. one high Conservation, one high Openness-to-Change; or opposed Self-Transcendence vs Self-Enhancement), that divergence is a strong, interpretable signal to apply LEAST-MISERY on value-polarising content — avoid celebratory-corruption or transgressive-amoral content for the Conservation/Self-Transcendence partner, avoid preachy-moralising content for the Self-Enhancement partner. Because Schwartz values sit on a circumplex, divergence has a natural geometry (opposite quadrants = genuine conflict; adjacent = compatible), which is more actionable than averaging arbitrary trait vectors. Caveat: the congruence-to-relationship-satisfaction evidence is about RELATIONSHIP outcomes, not about JOINT VIEWING satisfaction specifically — no located study tests which aggregation strategy predicts joint media enjoyment, so the strategy choice is an inference from the dyadic-values literature, not a validated joint-recommendation result. [uncertain]
quiz_ux_implications: The portrait/third-person format is a genuine UX advantage: by asking 'how much like you is THIS PERSON' rather than 'rate yourself', the PVQ partially launders social-desirability and self-presentation pressure, which is why it is preferred over the abstract Schwartz Value Survey for general and younger populations. It is suitable for fairly direct elicitation. Remaining biases: (1) acquiescence/yea-saying — all portraits are phrased positively, so a global tendency to agree inflates scores; standard fix is to ipsatise/centre each respondent's ratings around their own mean (Schwartz's recommended scoring), which a consumer onboarding must replicate or scores are uninterpretable. (2) social desirability survives for the most reputationally loaded values — Power ('It is important to him to be rich, to have a lot of money and expensive things') and Universalism/Benevolence are still pulled toward the flattering pole, though less than direct trait items. (3) Faking is plausible but low-stakes in an entertainment onboarding. Recommended format to minimise friction and maximise signal: keep the native portrait phrasing (it reads as a personality-quiz vibe users tolerate), use a 4-6 point 'like me' scale, and — important — apply within-person centring before scoring. For the Power/Self-Enhancement axis specifically, a forced-choice or 'this-or-that' framing ('Which character do you root for?') would reduce desirability pull versus a Likert agree-rating. The mandatory ipsatising step is the main implementation gotcha. [uncertain]
cross_cultural_validity: This is the Schwartz theory's flagship credential and a strong fit for HelpME2C's multilingual catalogue. The 10-value structure and circumplex have been validated across samples from 82 countries using the PVQ and the older SVS, spanning highly diverse geographic, linguistic, religious, age and occupational groups; the PVQ-21 specifically runs in 30+ countries via the European Social Survey. Crucially, validation explicitly includes non-Western and East-Asian samples, and Korean and Japanese value research using Schwartz's framework exists — relevant to a K-/J-content-heavy catalogue. The standard ceiling applies: the theorised circular STRUCTURE replicates widely (configural/structural validity is robust), but SCALAR measurement invariance across countries is frequently NOT met, so cross-NATIONAL mean comparisons are risky — although within-person value PRIORITIES (the rank-ordering of one user's own values after ipsatising) remain valid, which is exactly what a recommender needs (you compare a user's values to content, not to other countries' means). Documented breakdowns: ~5% of samples deviate substantially from the theorised pattern, with the most extreme deviations in sub-Saharan Africa, India, Malaysia, and rural/less-developed regions — so Hindi-content users and some Global-South contexts are a probable weak spot. Arabic-context coverage in the located invariance work is thinner/unconfirmed here. Net: materially better cross-culturally than any English-lexicon-only instrument, with India/sub-Saharan Africa flagged as the known soft spots. [uncertain]
privacy_consent_implications: High sensitivity — arguably HIGHER than trait models, and this is the field that most constrains adoption. Schwartz values are, by construction, the strongest known psychometric predictor of POLITICAL ORIENTATION and ideology (the values-to-voting link is the theory's most cited applied finding), and they index moral/religious orientation via Tradition, Conformity and Universalism. Storing a per-user value profile is therefore close to storing an inferred political-opinion and philosophical/religious-belief profile — squarely the kind of inference GDPR Article 9 treats as special-category data (political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs). This is a stronger Article 9 hook than for Big Five. Recommendation: treat collected value data as special-category-by-inference; require explicit, granular, purpose-limited consent; STRONGLY prefer transient use (compute a moral/political narrative-tone prior, then discard the raw value scores) or persist only a coarse, non-ideological derived content-preference signal — never store or expose a labelled 'this user is high Conservation / right-leaning' value. Re-identification and sensitive-inference risk are acute precisely because the data predicts political opinion. Intersects HelpME2C's ADR-0012 deletion/anonymisation rules and the CLAUDE.md §4 stop-and-ask on privacy-control flows; given the political-inference angle this should be a deliberate, documented decision, not a default. [uncertain] (exact Article 9 classification of inferred political-leaning from value scores is legally unsettled; treat conservatively.)
relationship_to_theme_vocabulary: Values map onto the MORAL, POWER, and WAR/SOCIETY theme clusters more directly than any trait model — this is the value-add. By higher-order dimension (music-genre evidence is the empirical anchor; theme mappings are reasoned extrapolation): HIGH SELF-TRANSCENDENCE (Universalism, Benevolence) -> found-family, friendship-tested, platonic-soulmates, grief-and-loss, civilian-under-occupation, war-and-its-toll, class-conflict (sympathetic-to-underdog framing), redemption-arc, betrayal-and-forgiveness, humanist character-study; lower appetite for celebratory cruelty. HIGH SELF-ENHANCEMENT (Power, Achievement) -> power-corrupts, rise-and-fall, heist-and-caper, underdog-rise, celebrity-and-fame, vigilante-justice, morally-grey ambitious protagonists, competition narratives; comfort rooting for the schemer. HIGH CONSERVATION (Security, Conformity, Tradition) -> restored-moral-order and earned comeuppance, workplace-procedural, mystery-investigation/whodunit (order-restoring), legacy-of-empire, parent-child-rift and family-centred drama, soldier-coming-home; likely AVERSION to dystopian-society, revolution-and-rebellion-as-triumph, and transgressive/absurdist content. HIGH OPENNESS-TO-CHANGE (Self-Direction, Stimulation) -> nonlinear-storytelling, magical-realism, absurdist-comedy, satire-of-power, revolution-and-rebellion, technology-runs-amok, alternate-history, first-contact, existential/cosmic-horror, identity-crisis; the music data ties this quadrant to 'Sophisticated' and 'Rebellious' preference. The two opposed AXES are the most useful product signal: Self-Transcendence-vs-Self-Enhancement separates humanist/justice narratives from ambition/power narratives, and Conservation-vs-Openness separates restored-order/comeuppance stories from boundary-pushing/transgressive ones — both axes index moral NARRATIVE TONE, which the Big Five barely touches. [uncertain]
implementation_recommendation: PARTIAL include, as the values complement to a personality core — medium priority, with a hard privacy gate. The case for inclusion is strong on signal: values give a moral/political narrative-tone prior the Big Five does not, map cleanly onto HelpME2C's moral/power theme clusters, transfer cross-medium, are cross-culturally well-validated, and — uniquely among the instruments researched — have direct evidence that value CONGRUENCE predicts dyadic satisfaction, which is the closest thing to validation of the two-person core use case. The case against is the privacy/political-inference exposure (see privacy_consent_implications), which is materially worse than for trait models. Recommendation if included: add 4-8 PVQ-style portrait items resolving only the two higher-order AXES (Self-Transcendence-vs-Self-Enhancement, Conservation-vs-Openness) — do NOT attempt 10- or 19-value resolution in onboarding; keep the native 'how like you is this person' phrasing; APPLY within-person centring (ipsatising) before scoring or the data is uninterpretable; place after the Big-Five-style core; and treat output as a transient moral-tone prior that is discarded or stored only as a coarse, non-ideological content-preference signal — never persisted as a political/value label. Dependencies: (1) only worth wiring once the moral/power theme cluster is in scoring (without it the value axes have nowhere to map); (2) best paired with, not instead of, a personality instrument — it complements Openness/Sensation-Seeking rather than replacing them; (3) requires the privacy/consent decision to be made deliberately under ADR-0012 before any storage. To exclude-for-now would be defensible purely on the political-inference risk; if so, the path back in is a validated transient-only pipeline plus the theme-cluster scoring. Strong recommendation either way: it is the best-positioned instrument here for the GROUP use case specifically. [uncertain]
key_sources: Schwartz (2012) 'An Overview of the Schwartz Theory of Basic Values' (Online Readings in Psychology and Culture; scholarworks.gvsu.edu — FREE) — canonical overview. Schwartz et al. (2001) 'Extending the Cross-Cultural Validity of the Theory of Basic Human Values with a Different Method of Measurement' (J. Cross-Cultural Psychology; introduces the PVQ; SAGE, paywalled; ResearchGate PDF free). European Social Survey 'The Human Values Scale (ESS)' and source questionnaire (europeansocialsurvey.org — FREE; PVQ-21 items + scoring). GESIS ZIS entry 'Human Values Scale (ESS)' Schwartz, Breyer, Danner (access.gesis.org/zis/524 — FREE). 'Can the PVQ21 Measure Schwartz's Refined Values?' (Journal of Personality Assessment 2026, Vol 108(2); tandfonline — paywalled) — short-form resolution limits. MUSIC link: Adolescent Musical Preferences and Schwartz's Basic Values, in an Open Book Publishers volume (openbookpublishers.com, obp.0389 ch.6 — FREE; N=1,358 Serbian adolescents). POLITICAL link: Caprara, Vecchione, Schwartz et al. (2017) 'Basic Values, Ideological Self-Placement, and Voting: A Cross-Cultural Study' (SAGE; paywalled) and Aspelund et al. (2013) Political Psychology (Wiley). DYADIC: Finnish couples value-vs-trait similarity study (ScienceDirect S0191886923002295, paywalled); van der Wal et al. (2024) 'Values in Romantic Relationships' (Pers. Soc. Psychol. Bulletin; SAGE). Cross-cultural invariance: 'Measurement equivalence of Schwartz's refined value structure... Estonia, Finland, Ethiopia' (ScienceDirect, paywalled); the 19-value 8-country invariance paper (PMC4157555 — FREE).
